title: Reactogenicity and safety of the human rotavirus vaccine, Rotarix™ in The Philippines, Sri Lanka, and India: a post-marketing surveillance study.
ehr_evidence: None mentioned; the study is described as a post-marketing surveillance study.
summary: A post-marketing surveillance study evaluating the reactogenicity and safety of the Rotarix™ rotavirus vaccine in infants in The Philippines, Sri Lanka, and India.
key_findings: 14.7% of infants in the Philippines, 14.9% in Sri Lanka, and 12.7% in India recorded at least Grade 2/3 solicited adverse events.
Most common solicited adverse events were irritability (Philippines and India) and fever (Sri Lanka).
41 serious adverse events (SAEs) were recorded in the Philippines, with 6 considered causally related to vaccination.
One vaccine-unrelated SAE occurred in a Sri Lankan infant.
population: infants in The Philippines, Sri Lanka, and India
limitations: Not specified in the provided text
